signals.idea: Two UX improvements: (1) Drop-to-create-track at the TOP of the timeline, not just the bottom. When dragging an item (external file, text tool, effect layer, or existing clip) near the top edge of the timeline, show a temporary drop zone above the first track. Dropping there creates a new track at position 0 and places the item on it. The existing tracks shift down to make room. This mirrors the existing bottom drop zone behavior. (2) Effect layer tab layout: the ClipPanel tabs (effects, timing, position, audio, text) should be evenly spaced across the full width regardless of how many are visible. Currently when only 2 tabs show (like for effect-layer clips), they're shoved to the left. They should stretch to fill the available width.

unresolved_flags.concern: Timeline top-drop: the plan does not implement the user's existing-clip case, so dragging an existing clip to the top edge would still create the new track at the bottom.

Timeline indicator plumbing: Step 3 understates the problem; restoring `TimelineCanvas` alone is not enough because the indicator path currently drops the top-vs-bottom state before it reaches the UI.
Timeline top-drop layout: the proposed top drop zone rendering is likely to destabilize hit-testing because it inserts new layout above the rows without adjusting row coordinate math.

signals.loop_summary: Iteration 1. Weighted score trajectory: 6.0. Plan deltas: n/a. Recurring critiques: 0. Resolved flags: 0. Open significant flags: 3.

items: Effect-layer clips (2 tabs) show tabs stretched evenly across full panel width, not bunched to the left.
All other clip types (3-tab audio, 4-tab visual/text) continue to render tabs correctly with even spacing.
Dragging any droppable item (file, text tool, effect layer, asset) above the first track shows a top drop indicator.
Dropping on the top indicator creates a new track at index 0 and shifts existing tracks down.
The bottom drop zone continues to work identically to before (no regression).
The top indicator label correctly reflects the track kind (visual vs audio) based on what's being dragged.

unresolved_flags.concern: Timeline top-drop: the plan does not implement the user's existing-clip case, so dragging an existing clip to the top edge would still create the new track at the bottom.
unresolved_flags.evidence: The idea explicitly includes existing clips. In the repo, internal clip drag uses `dropPosition.isNewTrack` in `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipDrag.ts:367-372`, and that path calls `createTrackAndMoveClip()`. `createTrackAndMoveClip()` appends via `addTrack(current.resolvedConfig, kind)` with no insertion index or top/bottom parameter in `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineTrackManagement.ts:145-158`. The plan only restores external-drop files and treats internal clip dragging as separate.

unresolved_flags.concern: Timeline indicator plumbing: Step 3 understates the problem; restoring `TimelineCanvas` alone is not enough because the indicator path currently drops the top-vs-bottom state before it reaches the UI.
unresolved_flags.evidence: `DropIndicatorPosition` does not define `trackId` or any top/bottom field in `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/DropIndicator.tsx:23-27`, while `DropIndicator` already reads `position?.trackId` in `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/DropIndicator.tsx:68-77`. Upstream, `toIndicatorPosition()` only forwards `isNewTrack` and `newTrackKind` from `DropPosition` in `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useDragCoordinator.ts:64-80`. So the plan's 'restore TimelineCanvas and keep the string match' approach misses the actual repo issue: the top-state is not plumbed through the coordinator/indicator path.

unresolved_flags.concern: Timeline top-drop layout: the proposed top drop zone rendering is likely to destabilize hit-testing because it inserts new layout above the rows without adjusting row coordinate math.
unresolved_flags.evidence: The HEAD implementation the plan wants to restore from commit `f93683867` inserts a normal-flow drop-zone `<div>` above `<DndContext>` in `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineCanvas.tsx`, i.e. inside the scroll content before the track rows. But `computeDropPosition()` still calculates row indices and `rowTop` from `editRect.top` with no extra top offset in `src/tools/video-editor/lib/drop-position.ts:97-110`. By inspection, once that bar renders it will push the first row down while drag hit-testing still assumes row 0 starts at the old origin.
